summary: Applied ADR 0225 on proxmox-host by bootstrapping a durable server-resident ansible-pull reconcile loop, minting a least-privilege Gitea read token for the host, correcting repeated host-local replays with a dedicated local ansible-pull inventory, publishing the exact rebased 0.177.43 integration candidate into the private Gitea source, and proving the timer-backed systemd path end to end through a successful latest-main host-local receipt.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest -q tests/test_server_resident_reconciliation.py` passed with `7 passed in 0.06s`, `ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/server-resident-reconciliation.yml --syntax-check` passed, and `./scripts/validate_repo.sh data-models architecture-fitness generated-docs agent-standards` passed from `/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.worktrees/ws-0225-live-apply` once the ADR index and generated truth surfaces were refreshed.

The isolated worktree replay installed `ansible-core`, the managed askpass helper, wrapper, and `lv3-server-resident-reconciliation.timer` on `proxmox-host`, mirrored the low-privilege Gitea token to `/etc/lv3/server-resident-reconciliation/gitea-read-token`, and later re-rendered `/var/lib/lv3/server-resident-reconciliation/ansible-pull-inventory.yml` plus the wrapper after the latest-main replay exposed an `ansible-pull` checkout mismatch on repeated runs.

After rebasing onto the latest `origin/main`, the private repo `ops/platform_server` published exact snapshot commit `5b121f700d0f1cd372ef85f24288691fb8a88e0c` for source commit `aacd8443b064aa2a14f9929f11a3ae45ae530095`, which made the final 0.177.43 integration candidate available to the trusted host-local `ansible-pull` runner without changing the source URL.

On `2026-03-28T17:53:04Z`, `sudo systemctl start lv3-server-resident-reconciliation.service` completed successfully on `proxmox-host`, `/srv/platform_server` checked out `5b121f700d0f1cd372ef85f24288691fb8a88e0c`, the working tree stayed clean, and `/var/lib/lv3/server-resident-reconciliation/receipts/latest.json` recorded `status: ok`, `exit_code: 0`, `duration_seconds: 9`, and `inventory_path: /var/lib/lv3/server-resident-reconciliation/ansible-pull-inventory.yml`.

notes: Gitea 1.25.5 accepted admin-token auth for user lifecycle calls but required basic auth as the restricted service identity for access-token listing, deletion, and creation, so the bootstrap now resets that restricted password only when minting or rotating the mirrored read token.

The trusted host-local source is the private Gitea repository, not GitHub origin. The final latest-main replay therefore published exact snapshot commit `5b121f700d0f1cd372ef85f24288691fb8a88e0c` for source commit `aacd8443b064aa2a14f9929f11a3ae45ae530095` before the last manual systemd replay.

Repeated host-local replays initially failed because `ansible-pull` reused the repo inventory for its own checkout step after the first run, which left no matching `localhost` or hostname target. The final fix renders `/var/lib/lv3/server-resident-reconciliation/ansible-pull-inventory.yml` so checkout and playbook execution both succeed on repeat runs.

This workstream became the final verified integration step, so the protected release and canonical-truth files were finalized on the same branch after the exact-main host-local ansible-pull verification completed and platform version 0.130.40 was recorded.
